enabled.notes: Main recommendations are always evaluated first

score_band_from_top.notes: Keep books within 2 points of the top score before variety rules

max_per_author.notes: Do not return more than 1 book per author in the main 3

prefer_different_era_section.notes: When scores are close, prefer different era_section values

prefer_higher_score_first.notes: Primary ordering still follows total score descending

enabled.notes: Optional extra recommendation beyond the main 3

label.notes: User-facing label for the wildcard slot

count.notes: Return at most one rogue(ish) pick

exclude_primary_results.notes: Cannot duplicate any of the main 3 results

different_author_from_primary.notes: Must not share an author with any of the main 3

prefer_different_era_section.notes: Prefer a different era_section from the main 3 when possible

min_theme_matches.notes: Must match at least one selected theme

must_match_selected_mood.notes: Must match the selected mood exactly

eligibility_core.notes: Core relevance gate before rogue(ish) selection

max_length_distance.notes: May be at most one length bucket away from the user selection

max_difficulty_distance.notes: May be at most one difficulty step away from the user selection

allow_exact_genre.notes: An exact genre match is eligible

allow_adjacent_genre.notes: An approved adjacent genre is also eligible

min_score_ratio_vs_top.notes: Must score at least 75% of the top overall score

prefer_lower_exposure.notes: Prefer books surfaced less often when multiple candidates are close

show_only_if_candidate_exists.notes: If no eligible candidate exists, return only the main 3

prefer_adjacent_genre_when_close.notes: Prefer an adjacent-genre rogue(ish) candidate when it is close enough to the best exact-genre rogue(ish) candidate.

adjacent_genre_preference_type.notes: Use adjacent genre as a controlled preference, not only as a tie-breaker. If the best adjacent-genre rogue(ish) candidate is within 1 point of the best exact-genre rogue(ish) candidate, choose the adjacent one.

adjacent_genre_max_score_gap.notes: Maximum allowed score gap between the best adjacent-genre rogue(ish) candidate and the best exact-genre rogue(ish) candidate for the adjacent one to be preferred. Set to 2 to allow more adjacent-genre rogue(ish) picks while keeping them close in quality.

max_result_count.notes: Maximum output is 3 primary results plus 1 rogue(ish) pick

rogueish_is_separate.notes: Display the rogue(ish) pick separately from the main 3

themes_added.notes: Added to theme answer set and scoring rules

satire_primary_mapping.notes: Satire now maps strongly in primary genre scoring
